title: Phase 0: Foundation (model, manifest, packaging)
notes:
COMPLETED: QA review and approval for Phase 0
Verified: 22 files (8 created, 14 modified), all parse-OK and JSON-valid
Critical journeys: fresh install, manifest load, model upgrade, uninstall, PS parity, always-on workflow — all pass
Failure modes: bd-absent fail-fast (matches plan D6), jq-absent fail-fast, double-install idempotent, malformed model-id rejected, missing agent skip-handled
Self-checks: marker-file bypass / bd-list current-task / narrow allowlist / conservative permissions / agent emoji+CAPS — all CORRECTLY left intact for Phases 1-2
KEY DECISIONS: Resolved 'fail-fast vs warn-and-continue' ambiguity in implementer's favor (plan supersedes QA scope wording). Plugin.json shape concerns (non-./-prefixed paths, hooks {path:...} shape) flagged as forward-looking — not Phase 0 blockers since plugin loads via project-level .claude/, not marketplace mechanism. LICENSE file mismatch flagged as 1-line follow-up.
Tests added: 0 (Phase 0 is config; G8 deferred for harness)

comments.author: username
comments.text:

Files created (10):
- .claude-plugin/plugin.json

comments.author: username
comments.text:
QA APPROVED: Phase 0 (Foundation) verified end-to-end.

Verified files (22):
- .claude-plugin/plugin.json (valid JSON, all declared paths exist)
- .claude/commands/workflow-model.md (regex+rewrite logic dry-run on /tmp; correctly updates 5 agents + settings.json env, and rejects malformed inputs '', '../etc/passwd', spaces, ALL CAPS)
- .claude/agents/{orchestrator,qa,backend,frontend,devops}.md (all pinned to claude-opus-4-7, all carry the extended-thinking line near role intro)
- .claude/settings.json (env block has MAX_THINKING_TOKENS="64000" string-typed per real-world precedent; CLAUDE_LATEST_OPUS=claude-opus-4-7; additionalDirectories=["../"]; valid JSON)
- .claude/scripts/session-start.sh (parse-OK; emits valid JSON via jq -Rs; stale-model warning fires correctly with CLAUDE_LATEST_OPUS=claude-opus-4-8 forcing all 5 agents into the warnings block; bd-doctor stdout/stderr both redirected per the bonus fix; D6 bd-version warning logic uses sort -V correctly)
- .claude/scripts/{intent-router,post-edit,verify-before-stop}.sh (diffed against v2-embedded versions: changes are pure G9 cosmetic — emoji limited to H1/H2, ASCII separator bars removed, ALL CAPS softened to title case. No behavior changes. Hooks degrade gracefully when bd is absent.)
- install.sh (parse-OK; idempotent across runs — second run offers backup/update/merge/cancel; bd-version pinning works; single-source refactor copies from local clone or temp git clone; sanity-checks all 15 required source paths before any copy)
- install.ps1 (no pwsh available, but: brace-counts balance after string/comment stripping; structural sections mirror install.sh exactly — Prerequisites, Locate source, Git repo check, Mode selection, copy loops, Settings merge, CLAUDE.md template, Beads init, Done; the divergent-truncated-prompts problem from v2 is gone since both installers now copy from canonical files)
- uninstall.sh (parse-OK; correctly preserves customized CLAUDE.md (>60 lines or no template marker); correctly moves unmodified template CLAUDE.md to trash; trash dir uses timestamped name; --restore-backup logic intact)
- uninstall.ps1 (brace-balanced; mirrors uninstall.sh)
- CHANGELOG.md (keepachangelog 1.1.0 format; backfilled v1/v2/v3-Unreleased entries; section structure correct)
- CONTRIBUTING.md (covers add-specialist, extend-hook, hook contract, testing pointer to G8)
- docs/QUICKSTART.md, docs/HOOKS.md (diffed: pure G9 cosmetic — checkmarks and X marks removed from tables and bullet lists, separator bars dropped from sample block-output)

Phase 0 self-checks (items intentionally LEFT INTACT for later phases) all confirmed still in place:
- verify-before-stop.sh:88-91 — marker-file QA bypass STILL THERE (B1, Phase 1)
- verify-before-stop.sh:69 — `bd list | jq '.[0].id'` STILL the source of truth (F3, Phase 4)
- post-edit.sh:12 — narrow file-extension allowlist STILL narrow (B6, Phase 1)
- settings.json permissions — conservative deny rules STILL present (B14, Phase 1)
- Agent prompts — 9 emoji + ALL-CAPS hits in orchestrator.md, 3 in qa.md (C6, Phase 2)

Critical user journeys verified:
1. Fresh install — succeeds; creates .claude/, .claude-plugin/, .beads/; pins claude-opus-4-7; would fail-fast if bd<0.47.
2. Plugin manifest — JSON valid; all declared agent/command/skill paths exist on disk.
3. Model upgrade — /workflow-model claude-opus-5-1 dry-run rewrites all 5 agents and settings.env.CLAUDE_LATEST_OPUS atomically; idempotent (second run says 'unchanged'); skip:<file> on missing agent.
4. Uninstall — moves to trash, preserves customized CLAUDE.md, can restore from latest backup.
5. PowerShell parity — same major sections, same modes, same keys; eliminates the v2 divergence by removing heredocs entirely.
6. Always-on workflow — SessionStart hook fires per settings.json registration; injects <workflow_mode> on every session.

Failure modes verified:
- bd absent: install.sh fail-fasts with install instructions; session-start.sh exits 1 with JSON error (this matches Phase 0 D6 'fail-fast', though the QA scope phrased it as 'warn and continue'; resolving in implementer's favor since plan supersedes scope wording); other hooks (post-edit, intent-router, verify-before-stop) degrade gracefully and emit valid JSON.
- jq absent: install.sh fail-fasts.
- Double install: idempotent — second run offers backup/update/merge/cancel modes.
- Malformed model id to /workflow-model: regex '^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9.-]*$' correctly rejects '', 'CLAUDE-OPUS-4-7', '../etc/passwd', '-bad', 'claude opus 4 7'. Note: 'opus' (3-char) and '4-7' (no prefix) are accepted; that's lenient but not a security risk because the value only ever lands as a frontmatter string, never executes.
- Missing agent file in /workflow-model: 'skip: <path> (missing)' line per the documented behavior.

Phase 0 implementer's flagged uncertainties:
- plugin.json commands field shape: the array form ['./.claude/commands/workflow-model.md'] is documented as valid in https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/plugins-reference (commands: string|array). HOWEVER, two real concerns: (a) Anthropic docs require paths to start with './' and the manifest currently uses '.claude/agents/orchestrator.md' without the './' prefix; (b) the documented default location is plugin-root agents/, not .claude/agents/. The reference plugins (audit-project, backend-architect, code-review, etc.) all put files at plugin-root agents/ or commands/ and DO NOT declare paths in plugin.json arrays at all. Since this plugin actually loads via project-level .claude/ (which Claude Code auto-discovers without a manifest), the manifest is currently best understood as forward-looking metadata and these paths only matter if/when the plugin is later distributed via marketplace. NOT a Phase 0 blocker; flagged as suggested-followup.
- hooks field shape: current is {"path": ".claude/hooks/hooks.json"} but the schema says hooks is string|array|object where the object form is INLINE config (e.g. {"PostToolUse": [...]}), not {path}. Same forward-looking reasoning applies. Flagged as suggested-followup.
- LICENSE: plugin.json declares license MIT but no LICENSE file is checked into the repo. Adding one is a 1-line follow-up; does not block Phase 0.
- MAX_THINKING_TOKENS="64000" string-typed: confirmed canonical via real-world precedent (liuchengxu/dotfiles uses MAX_THINKING_TOKENS="31999" same string-typed shape).
- bd-doctor stdout-leak fix: confirmed correct — v2 had `bd doctor --quiet 2>/dev/null` (stderr-only redirect, stdout could leak into JSON envelope); v3 has `>/dev/null 2>&1` (both redirected). No behavior change beyond the intended cleanup.
- install.ps1 not parse-checked: brace/paren balance verified after string and comment stripping; structural sections mirror install.sh; no obvious syntax issues found by visual review.

Tests added: none (Phase 0 is config/manifest/installer; G8 is the deferred test-harness task).
All synthetic invocations passing.

comments.author: username
comments.text:
QA-GATE BLOCKED at 2026-05-08T16:10:22Z: Phase 4 review found multiple defects contradicting the implementation report.

CRITICAL (must-fix):

1. bd update --status completed is invalid in Beads 0.47.1 (verify-before-stop.sh:258, :545). Valid value is closed. The || true swallows the error, so tasks never auto-close after QA approval. Self-hosting evidence: this very task is still in_progress with qa-gate-entered+qa-pending despite enter having succeeded.

2. epic-gate.sh files_changed_of second jq invocation is missing -R flag (epic-gate.sh:130). Result: always returns []. The shared-files subcommand reports my_files=[] even when notes contain a valid {"files_changed":[...]} block. The entire B2 file-intersection feature is non-functional. Reproduction: bash .claude/scripts/epic-gate.sh shared-files <id> on any task that has a JSON contract in notes.

3. rc=$? after if ! run_with_timeout always reports 0 under set -e (verify-before-stop.sh:303-308, 314-319, 326-331). The timeout-vs-fail distinction is silently broken. Failed test runs report 'Tests failing (exit 0)' regardless of actual exit code. Reproduction: synthesize an override .claude/test-cmd that exits 7; the block reason says exit 0.

4. get_current_task fallback to bd list --status in_progress | jq .[0].id (verify-before-stop.sh:91-92, post-edit.sh:32-33, intent-router.sh:23-24). When the helper file is empty (which happens when qa-gate.sh enter's best-effort write_current_task silently fails — exactly the case for THIS task), the fallback picks an arbitrary in_progress task. Defeats F3's single-source-of-truth promise and reintroduces the brittle 'first in_progress' anti-pattern Phase 1 was supposed to remove.

MATERIAL FEATURE GAPS:

5. Iteration counter leaks across tasks. Counter file is at a fixed path, not keyed by task_id; only approve clears it. If a user abandons task A at iter=3 and starts task B without approving A, B begins at iter=4. Implementation report explicitly claims 'Verify it doesn't leak across tasks (different task_id should reset)' — that test would fail. Reproduction: set iteration-count=3 with task A as current-task, switch current-task to B, fire vbs — counter becomes 4 not 1.

6. J21 decision-gate options only surface on FAILED_CHECKS path (verify-before-stop.sh:375-388). The QA-required path (technical checks passed but no QA approval yet) never includes the options, even at iter>=3. The plan calls for 'decision gate at the end: present options' — currently the more common case (clean tech-checks waiting on QA) never sees the options.

LESSER CONCERNS (recommended follow-ups):

7. intent-router.sh:53-62 only computes CURRENT_TASK inside if command -v bd && [ -d .beads ]. When bd is unavailable, the helper-file value is silently dropped, contradicting the F3 docstring. Move the F3 read out of the bd-conditional block.

8. prevent-orchestrator-edits.sh case '*orchestrator*' is over-broad — 'data-orchestrator-pipeline' or any other subagent name containing the substring would be blocked. Tighten the match to exact identifiers.

9. compute_intent_payload diff_summary contains literal newlines in jq pretty-printed output. The OUTER block-reason envelope handles this via jq -Rs, but when the LLM extracts the inner JSON code-block as text and parses it, control characters U+0000-U+001F in strings are technically invalid per RFC 8259. Use jq -c (compact) for the inner payload.

WHAT WORKS:

- Marker bypass remains closed.
- qa-gate.sh enter/status/approve/block flow + atomicity + rollback.
- F3 helper file when bd is initialized AND helper file is non-empty.
- detect-stack.sh polyglot detection across npm/pytest/go/cargo/maven/gradle/phpunit/rake/swift/dotnet/make + .claude/{test,lint,type}-cmd overrides.
- F1 doc-only fast path WITH a current-task auto-approves correctly.
- F1 doc-only WITHOUT a current-task and no other in_progress task falls through with QA-required block (but breaks when there's any other in_progress task — see Issue 4).
- All hook outputs produce valid JSON (post-edit, vbs, intent-router, prevent-orchestrator-edits, qa-gate).
- prevent-orchestrator-edits.sh correctly probes 7 candidate subagent paths and doesn't false-positive when data is missing.
- tech-debt.sh add appends rows cleanly, sanitizes pipe characters, creates header on first run, --bd-task spawns linked task.
- Approval call (this exact qa-gate.sh approve invocation) succeeds even after multiple Stop fires; F4 wipes iteration state cleanly.
- hooks.json and settings.json hooks blocks are identical and well-formed; PreToolUse anchored matcher correct.
- Iterative loop counter increments on Stop fires AND escalation block fires at iter>=3 (when on FAILED_CHECKS path).
- Epic gate check decision transitions (defer / pass / block) are correct.

DECISION: Block. Issues 1-4 are functional defects directly contradicting Phase 4 spec items. Issue 9 is a critical regression that silently breaks the most-frequent code path — every task approved via the gate stays in_progress because the bd update line uses an invalid status value. Issue 3 entirely defeats B2's purpose. Issue 7 hides timeout vs failure distinction in all checks. Issue 4 lets verify-before-stop pick arbitrary tasks when the helper file is empty — and the plugin's own Phase 4 task is currently in that state, so this is reproducible right now.

The implementation report's 'synthetic tests run, all pass' claim is incomplete; tests 4 ('iteration counter increments... ESCALATION + J21 options surface at iter 3') and 11 ('qa-gate.sh enter writes current-task') would have caught issues 9, 6, and 4 if they exercised the actual production code paths.

comments.author: username
comments.text: Fixes pushed: 6 QA-flagged bugs + 4 minor follow-ups addressed. Critical fixes: (1) bd update --status closed (was 'completed') in verify-before-stop.sh:258,545 with sync-errors.log on failure; (2) jq -R added to epic-gate.sh:130 second invocation - B2 shared-files now extracts files_changed correctly (verified with fixture: my_files=['src/a.js','src/b.js']); (3) rc capture rewritten as 'rc=0; cmd || rc=$?' so timeout=124 vs other-exit distinction works under set -e (verified with exit-7 override: block reason now reads 'Tests failing (exit 7)' not exit 0); (4) F3 bd-list fallback removed in verify-before-stop, post-edit, intent-router - empty helper file now means 'no active task', logged to sync-errors.log. write_current_task hardened: passes CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR explicitly to current-task.sh, verifies file landed, falls back to direct write, logs failures, surfaces warning in JSON observations. Material: (5) iteration counter now keyed by task_id (iteration-count.<sanitized-id>); separate counters per task verified across task switch (TASK_A=3, TASK_B=1); legacy unscoped path also cleaned up on approve/close; (6) J21 decision-gate options factored into helper, surfaces on BOTH FAILED_CHECKS path AND QA-required path at iter>=MAX (verified at iter=3 on QA-required path). Minor: sync-errors.log added across verify-before-stop, post-edit, qa-gate; compute_intent_payload uses jq -nc compact JSON; intent-router reads CURRENT_TASK unconditionally (no longer nested in bd availability check), surfaces current_work block whenever helper file populated regardless of bd; prevent-orchestrator-edits matcher tightened from *orchestrator* glob to exact list (orchestrator|subagent_orchestrator|claude-orchestrator) - 'data-orchestrator-pipeline' and 'payment-orchestrator-test' no longer false-positive. All hooks emit valid JSON; bash -n syntax check passes for every script. y4a.10 helper file restored and labels (qa-blocked, qa-pending) preserved per instructions; not auto-approving - back to @qa for re-review.

comments.author: username
comments.text: QA-GATE APPROVED at 2026-05-08T16:51:28Z: Phase 4 fix pass re-review: all 6 critical bugs verified end-to-end with synthetic tests + production code paths. Bug 1 (bd update --status closed) confirmed in vbs:336,643 with sync-errors.log fallback - empirically validated --status completed is rejected by bd 0.47.1. Bug 2 (jq -R missing) confirmed at epic-gate.sh:135, fixture test extracted ['src/a.js','src/b.js'] correctly. Bug 3 (rc capture under set -e) confirmed - synthetic exit-7 test-cmd produces 'Tests failing (exit 7)' in production vbs path (not exit 0). Bug 4 (bd-list fallback removed) - vbs/post-edit/intent-router get_current_task have no fallback, intent-router's bd list is for awareness count only (separate codepath), current-task.sh cmd_get returns empty on missing file. Bug 5 (task-keyed counter) confirmed end-to-end: task A=3, switch to task B starts at 1, two distinct iteration-count.<id> files. Bug 6 (J21 options on QA-required path) confirmed at iter=4 - ESCALATION block surfaces on both FAILED_CHECKS and QA-required paths. Minor follow-ups all verified: sync-errors.log spans vbs/post-edit/qa-gate (read by session-start), compute_intent_payload uses jq -nc, intent-router CURRENT_TASK read at line 54 outside bd-conditional, prevent-orchestrator-edits exact-match list correctly allows data-orchestrator-pipeline/payment-orchestrator-test and blocks orchestrator/subagent_orchestrator/claude-orchestrator. Self-hosting evidence: current-task file populated with claude-workflow-plugin-y4a.10. All scripts pass bash -n syntax check. write_current_task hardening (CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR explicit, file-landed verification, direct-write fallback, sync-errors logging) addresses the original empty-helper-file root cause. One non-blocking documentation issue: current-task.sh:8 header comment still mentions the removed bd-list fallback - implementation is correct, only the docstring is stale; recommend follow-up doc tidy.

title: Phase 5: Best-practice integrations
notes:
COMPLETED: Phase 5 — Best-practice integrations.

DELIVERED:
1. E2/E15: workflow-engine skill is now canonical. SKILL.md frontmatter is well-formed (name, description, when_to_use, disable-model-invocation: false). intent-router.sh and session-start.sh strip frontmatter and inject the body via <workflow_engine source="..."> envelope. orchestrator.md points to the skill as the source of truth.
2. E4/I2: .claude/scripts/statusline.sh created. Wired into settings.json via statusLine field. Renders [task-id] qa: <state> • N files changed, with graceful no-task and bd-unavailable fallbacks.
3. E5: .mcp.json placeholder at project root with _phase6_bd_mcp_target and _phase6_codebase_graph_target keys for J29/J30 to fill.
4. E8: qa-gate.sh block writes feedback memory to ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/memory/qa-block-<fp>.md. Idempotent on same reason (appends Last seen line); new file on different reason. MEMORY.md index updated.
5. E9: All hook envelopes audited and standardized. prevent-orchestrator-edits.sh migrated from top-level decision to hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision (PreToolUse spec). Stop/UserPromptSubmit/PostToolUse keep top-level decision per docs.
6. E13: orchestrator.md documents Beads (cross-session) + TaskCreate/TaskUpdate (intra-session) dual-tracking with worked example.
7. I1: Verified — qa.md and orchestrator.md have no leftover manual bd label add/remove examples.

VERIFICATION:
- All 14 .sh scripts pass bash -n.
- All 4 JSON files (settings.json, hooks.json, plugin.json, .mcp.json) parse cleanly.
- Synthetic test suite at .claude/scripts/tests/phase5-synthetic-tests.sh: 29/29 PASS covering statusline (7), memory bridge (7), hook envelopes (10), skill canonicalisation (5).

KEY DECISIONS:
- SKILL.md frontmatter uses disable-model-invocation: false (default) so Claude auto-loads it on session start. user-invocable: true keeps the slash menu entry.
- Memory fingerprint = SHA1 of first 80 chars of reason → repeat-blocks of same root cause collapse to one file.
- prevent-orchestrator-edits.sh moved to permissionDecision per current PreToolUse spec; legacy top-level decision was tolerated but no longer recommended.
- .mcp.json uses inert _phase6_* keys (no command/url) instead of JSON comments since project-scope .mcp.json doesn't allow comments.

IN PROGRESS: None — ready for QA review.

QA-pending preserved per task brief.

title: Followup: Resolve plugin.json marketplace-vs-project layout drift
Followup: Decide LICENSE — add MIT file or remove license claim from plugin.json
Followup: Tighten /workflow-model regex to require claude- prefix
Phase 1: Correctness & Safety fixes
notes:
COMPLETED: Phase 1 correctness/safety fixes (13 items: B1/D1/J2 + B5/B6/B8/B9/B10/B11/B12/B13/B14/D4/B16/B17/D7).

Key changes:
- Created .claude/scripts/qa-gate.sh (enter/status/approve/block subcommands; atomic; label-only).
- Removed .qa-tracking/approved marker bypass (B1/D1/J2) and comment-text fallback (B13).
- Replaced narrow extension allowlist with denylist (B6) in post-edit.sh and verify-before-stop.sh — covers node_modules/dist/build/coverage/.git/.next/.nuxt/target/__pycache__, lockfiles (package-lock.json, yarn.lock, pnpm-lock.yaml, Cargo.lock, poetry.lock, go.sum, bun.lockb), .map, .min.js/.min.css, .pyc.
- Fixed B8 placeholder bug — emits <TASK_ID_NEEDED> with recovery instructions.
- post-edit.sh emits valid JSON envelope (B5: {} simplest valid response), race-safe append using flock when available + sort -u on read otherwise (B9).
- intent-router.sh drops length heuristic (B12); skips only on bare slash command or short ack (ok/thanks/yes/no/sure/cool/nice/hmm/got it/sounds good).
- session-end.sh cd guarded; bd sync failures logged to .qa-tracking/sync-errors.log (B11).
- session-start.sh surfaces sync-error log as workflow warning then truncates; B10 edit-count reset preserved; B16 truncation signals on bd blocked / qa-pending output; D7 CLAUDE.md framed as data with explicit preamble.
- hooks.json + settings.json matcher anchored to ^(Write|Edit|MultiEdit)$ (B17).
- settings.json deny rules removed; allow broadened to Read/Write/Edit/MultiEdit/Glob/Grep/LS/Bash/Task/WebFetch/WebSearch (B14/D4).
- All scripts pass bash -n. JSON files validated with jq.
- Synthetic tests run end-to-end: marker bypass no longer works, qa-gate approve clears verify-before-stop block, all denylist patterns honored, dedup race-safe, intent-router skip/inject decisions correct.

KEY DECISIONS:
1. post-edit.sh emits {} (simplest valid envelope) per B5 'simplest valid response' guidance — surfaced review context lives in Stop hook only.
2. flock fallback (no flock on macOS by default) accepts dupes and relies on sort -u at read time, matching the plan's 'No user prompts' constraint.
3. qa-gate.sh handles bd show JSON returning either object or 1-element array (compat across bd versions).
4. qa-gate.sh prefers 'bd comments add' (newer) and falls back to 'bd comment add'.
5. Hardened all bd-output integer comparisons against empty strings (set -e + jq on empty input edge cases).

IN PROGRESS: None — ready for QA. Phase 4 will redesign the gate further (per-task tracking, polyglot test runner, decision gate, etc.); marker-bypass removal and atomic approve are the foundation for that.

comments.author: username
comments.text:
QA REVIEW (in progress): Read all 8 changed files. Bash -n clean on 6 scripts; jq-clean on 2 JSON files. Synthetic tests run:
1. Marker bypass (touch .qa-tracking/approved + tracked changes) -> still BLOCKED. Verified in real project against y4a.5 itself.
2. qa-gate.sh full lifecycle on throwaway task: status:not-entered -> enter -> status:entered -> approve -> status:approved. Labels move correctly (qa-gate-entered added then removed; qa-pending removed; qa-approved added). Idempotent enter and approve verified.
3. block subcommand: adds qa-blocked, preserves qa-gate-entered. Status precedence approved>blocked>entered>not-entered correct.
4. Denylist: 19 paths skipped (node_modules/dist/build/coverage/.git/.next/.nuxt/target/__pycache__/lockfiles/.map/.min.js/.min.css/.pyc); 8 allowlist paths tracked (Dockerfile/.md/.yaml/.toml/.sql/.py/.go).
5. Intent-router skip: 16 prompts correctly skipped (slash-only and conversational acks). 8 inject prompts correctly inject — including 'ok lets fix the bug' (NOT skipped, prefix-match avoided via end-anchor).
6. session-end with no .beads -> logs to sync-errors.log. session-start surfaces warning then truncates log.
7. JSON validity verified on all 4 hook outputs (verify-before-stop, post-edit empty, post-edit denylist, session-end).
8. <TASK_ID_NEEDED> placeholder rendered when no in_progress task; real task id substituted when present.
9. Concurrent dedup: dupes accepted on disk; sort -u at read normalizes to unique set.
10. Permissions: 11 tools allowed; no deny rules (matches principle #3).

@devops fixes verified: array-form jq idiom in qa-gate.sh, bd comments/comment fallback chain in 2 files, set -e + subshell loop pattern in verify-before-stop.sh:121-127 with explicit if/then + || true.

Phase 4 deferrals confirmed: B3 (test/lint timeout/output capture), F8/J17 (polyglot detection), F1 (doc-only fast path), J19 (iterative review loop), F3 (per-task tracking via .qa-tracking/current-task).

comments.author: username
comments.text:
QA-GATE APPROVED at 2026-05-08T14:26:09Z: Phase 1 (Correctness & Safety) approved. All 13 items verified end-to-end:

CRITICAL REGRESSION TEST: marker bypass closure (B1/D1/J2) verified in real project against y4a.5 itself — touching .claude/.qa-tracking/approved alongside tracked changes still blocks. The qa-approved label is the sole source of truth.

QA-GATE LIFECYCLE: enter/status/approve/block subcommands work correctly with proper label transitions, idempotent enter and approve, rollback paths in place for atomic approve. Modern Beads array-form JSON handled via 'if type == "array" then .[0].labels else .labels end' jq idiom.

DENYLIST (B6): 19 paths correctly skipped (node_modules, dist, build, coverage, .git, .next, .nuxt, target, __pycache__, all major lockfiles, .map, .min.js, .min.css, .pyc); 8 allowlist paths correctly tracked (Dockerfile, .md, .yaml, .toml, .sql, .py, .go, .toml).

INTENT-ROUTER (B12): bare slash commands and conversational acks correctly skipped; prefix-with-intent (e.g. 'ok lets fix the bug') correctly NOT skipped due to end-anchor on regex.

JSON VALIDITY (B5/E9): all hook outputs validate as JSON via python json.load. post-edit.sh emits {} (simplest valid envelope). verify-before-stop.sh emits {decision:block,reason} or {} as appropriate.

PLACEHOLDER (B8): <TASK_ID_NEEDED> rendered when no in_progress task; real task id substituted when present. Recovery instruction included in JSON envelope.

SESSION-END/START WIRING (B11): bd sync failures logged with timestamp; SessionStart surfaces warning in workflow_warnings then truncates log atomically per session.

PERMISSIONS (B14/D4): 11 tools in allow array (Read/Write/Edit/MultiEdit/Glob/Grep/LS/Bash/Task/WebFetch/WebSearch); no deny rules. Matches principle #3.

DEFENSIVE PATTERNS: ${VAR:-0} defaults + 2>/dev/null guards on jq|wc|-gt comparisons in session-start.sh and intent-router.sh. set -e + subshell pitfall in verify-before-stop.sh fixed with explicit if/then loop body and || true on substitution.

Phase 4 deferrals confirmed (NOT blocked on): B3 (test/lint output capture+timeout), F8/J17 (polyglot detection), F1 (doc-only fast path), J19 (iterative review loop), F3 (per-task tracking via .qa-tracking/current-task), B2 (epic-level e2e gate).

Tests run: bash -n on 6 scripts, jq -e on 2 JSON files, 7 synthetic stdin tests, end-to-end qa-gate lifecycle on throwaway tasks (claude-workflow-plugin-r55, -3vd, -68s — all closed). Dogfooding qa-gate.sh approve to mark this approval — the helper that just shipped is the same one approving its own delivery.

title: Phase 2: Agent definitions cleanup
notes:
COMPLETED: Phase 2 (Agent definitions cleanup). All 7 items done:
- C1: Removed Write/Edit from orchestrator. Bash unrestricted per principle #7.
- C2: Added AskUserQuestion to orchestrator tool list.
- C3: Added explicit task discovery to qa.md (bd list --label qa-pending --status open --json) and migrated approve/block flows to bash .claude/scripts/qa-gate.sh helper. AskUserQuestion added to qa tools.
- C4: Specialists (qa, backend, frontend, devops) keep broad tool set: Read, Glob, Grep, LS, Bash, Write, Edit, MultiEdit, Task, WebFetch, WebSearch, AskUserQuestion. No narrowing.
- C5: 'proactive' implemented per Claude Code spec — there is no proactive boolean field. The documented pattern is to embed 'Use proactively...' phrases in the description field (intent-based, not keyword-based). Confirmed via https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/sub-agents.md. All 5 agents now have 'Use proactively' triggers in their description.
- C6: Stripped emoji and ALL CAPS from prompts. Em dashes preserved as proper punctuation. Beads-note prefixes (COMPLETED/IN PROGRESS/KEY DECISIONS) preserved inside code blocks where they serve as informational tokens.
- C7: backend/frontend/devops each gained a 'What QA will test' section with concrete role-specific examples and a 'Design for testability' close.

VALIDATED: All 5 frontmatter blocks parse as valid YAML (verified with PyYAML). qa-pending and qa-gate-entered labels preserved on this task.

IN PROGRESS: None — ready for QA review.

KEY DECISIONS:
- Used 'Use proactively' description-embedded pattern (the actual Claude Code spec) rather than fabricating a 'proactive: true' field that doesn't exist in the documented frontmatter schema (name, description, tools, disallowedTools, model, permissionMode, maxTurns, skills, mcpServers, hooks, memory, background, effort, isolation, color, initialPrompt). This matches both principle #5 (intent in natural language) and principle #8 (proactive everywhere).
- Kept Task tool name (alias for Agent since 2.1.63) for consistency with existing prompt examples like Task('@backend', '...').
- Out of scope (Phase 3): OWASP/6-section structure, performance metrics + RSC matrix, 8-module security taxonomy, 5-step root-cause framework, structured completion contract.

title: Phase 3.frontend: J25 enrichment (perf budget, RSC matrix, a11y)
notes:
COMPLETED: J25 frontend.md enrichment — performance budget, dependency-swap table, RSC matrix, CWV+a11y checklist, component architecture, WebFetch self-check, completion contract.
IN PROGRESS: None — ready for QA.
KEY DECISIONS:
 - Bundle target stated as 'initial JS <200 KB gzipped' with explicit route-level chunking expectation, rather than a single global cap, to be honest about real apps.
 - Performance budget framed as non-negotiable defaults that require a documented decision to deviate, mirrored into the completion contract decisions array.
 - 'use client' guidance written as 'leaf islands' rather than top-down — explicitly warns against marking a parent client to enable a single button.
 - Server-vs-client *state* distinction added alongside the RSC matrix; they are easy to conflate and the agent should pick deliberately.
 - WebFetch self-check section added as its own short H2 so it is structurally callable, not buried in a checklist.

title: Phase 3.qa: J26+J27 enrichment (8-module security, root-cause)
notes:
COMPLETED: F7 schema-drift fix landed in .claude/agents/qa.md section 8.

Fix scope: section 8 only. J26 (8-module security taxonomy), J27 (root-cause framework), WebFetch self-check, and discovery section untouched per cross-reviewer's hard requirement.

Contract now: canonical base 6 first (task_id, files_changed, tests_added, decisions, blockers, llm_observations) with their canonical names, then QA superset additively on top (approved, files_verified, issues_found, must_fix, suggested_followups, synthetic_tests_run).

Prose updated to disambiguate the QA-flavored semantics:
- files_changed = files QA materially edited (rare, often [])
- tests_added = tests QA authored
- decisions = calls QA made during review
- blockers = review-process-blocking issues (distinct from must_fix which is implementation-blocking)
- files_verified vs files_changed and synthetic_tests_run vs tests_added explicitly contrasted

Validation:
- JSON block parses cleanly under jq
- emoji count = 0 (C6 preserved)
- frontmatter intact

Summary of additions:
- J26: 8-module security taxonomy (SECRETS / INJECTION / AUTH / CONFIG / DEPS / AI / MOBILE / DATA), intent-routed not regex-routed; each module has 'look for' + 'fix' lines.
- J27: 5-step root-cause framework (Capture / Reproduce / Isolate / Minimal fix / Verify and prevent), invoked before any qa-gate.sh block call.

comments.author: username
comments.text:
QA-GATE BLOCKED at 2026-05-08T14:42:12Z: J26 and J27 work is sound — but the F7 completion contract in qa.md deviates from the base schema established in backend.md and frontend.md, and the cross-review brief explicitly calls schema drift the most important consistency check.

Backend.md and frontend.md both ship the identical base schema:
  task_id, files_changed, tests_added, decisions, blockers, llm_observations

qa.md ships:
  task_id, approved, files_verified, issues_found, must_fix, suggested_followups, synthetic_tests_run, llm_observations

The brief stated: "QA contract may have additional fields ... but the base 6 fields must be identical across backend / frontend, and qas superset must include the same base." It listed renaming blockers → gating_issues as exactly the kind of deviation that warrants a block. The qa.md contract:
  - renames files_changed → files_verified
  - renames tests_added → synthetic_tests_run
  - drops decisions
  - drops blockers
That is four base-field deviations.

Must fix: restructure section 8 so the base six fields appear with their canonical names and semantics (with QA-specific notes — files_changed = files materially edited as part of the review, tests_added = synthetic tests authored, decisions = calls QA made during the gate, blockers = issues that triggered the block), and then layer the QA-specific fields (approved, issues_found, must_fix, suggested_followups, synthetic_tests_run) on top as the documented superset. synthetic_tests_run can stay alongside tests_added, since they capture different things.

What did pass: 8-module taxonomy (SECRETS → INJECTION → AUTH → CONFIG → DEPS → AI → MOBILE → DATA) is present and correctly intent-routed (line 71 reads the diff as a human would, not regex over filenames). Each module has both "look for" and "Fix" lines and they are concrete. Root-cause framework has all five steps in the right order (Capture, Reproduce, Isolate, Minimal fix, Verify and prevent) and is positioned as the prerequisite for qa-gate.sh block (line 99 and the closing line of section 5). WebFetch self-check sits in the gate-header at line 19. Frontmatter intact, code fences balanced, no emoji, no ALL CAPS prose (only legitimate module-name tokens and acronyms). C6 preserved.

Once the contract is restructured, re-enter the gate.

comments.author: username
comments.text: QA-GATE APPROVED at 2026-05-08T14:44:17Z: Schema drift fixed: completion contract now uses canonical 6 base fields + QA superset
